title: Add Ones Place Values
description: Add the ones digits of two two-digit numbers.
condition: Two two-digit numbers expressed in tens and ones; focus on ones digits.
response: Add the ones digits to find the total number of ones.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Assumes no regrouping in given item.

title: Add Tens Place Values
description: Add the tens digits of two two-digit numbers.
condition: Two two-digit numbers expressed in tens and ones; focus on tens digits.
response: Add the tens digits to find the total number of tens.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Assumes understanding that 2 represents 20 and 1 represents 10.

title: Add Two Categories from Graph
description: Find the total of two categories by reading their values from a graph and adding them.
condition: Two categories identified in a bar graph with known numerical values.
response: Add the two values to find the combined total.
label: Principle / Model
example: If cherry is 6 and peach is 13, compute $6 + 13 = 19$.

title: Add Two Numbers Within 20
description: Compute the sum of two whole numbers within 20.
condition: Expression of the form a + b where a and b are whole numbers within 20.
response: State the correct sum a + b.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compose Number from Tens and Ones
description: Combine computed tens and ones to form a standard two-digit number.
condition: A quantity expressed as some tens and some ones.
response: Combine tens and ones into a single number.
label: Principle / Model
example: 3 tens and 8 ones combine to make $38$.

title: Count Marks in Picture Graph
description: Count the number of symbols (e.g., dots) in a category of a picture graph to determine the total.
condition: Given a picture graph where each symbol represents one student.
response: Count all symbols in the specified category and state the total.
label: Production / Skill
example: If there are 9 dots above dog, then 9 students chose dogs.

title: Decompose Subtrahend to Make a Ten
description: Break apart a number being subtracted to first reach the nearest ten.
condition: Subtraction within 20 where the minuend is above a multiple of ten (e.g., 15 − 6).
response: Decompose the subtrahend into parts to subtract to the nearest ten, then subtract the remainder.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Strategy-based KC emphasizing making a ten.

title: Find Difference Using Subtraction
description: To find how many more or fewer one quantity is than another, subtract the smaller quantity from the larger quantity.
condition: Two quantities are given and the problem asks 'how many more' or 'how many fewer'.
response: Compute larger − smaller to find the difference.
label: Principle / Model
example: Clare spent 18 minutes and Diego spent 8 minutes. The difference is $18 - 8 = 10$.

title: Identify Category on Graph or Table
description: Locate the named category (e.g., dog, biking) in a graph or table before reading or representing its value.
condition: Given a question naming a specific category in a graph or table.
response: Select or reference the matching labeled category.
label: Category
example: To answer about dogs, locate the column labeled "dog."
notes: Precursor step to counting or drawing.

title: Interpret Compare Tape Diagram
description: Identify that a tape diagram represents a comparison when one bar shows a known amount and an extra segment represents how many more or fewer.
condition: A tape diagram shows two bars of different lengths with a question mark on the extra portion.
response: Recognize the diagram represents a difference between the two quantities.
label: Concept
example: A bar labeled 30 and a longer bar labeled 45 with an extra segment marked '?' shows $30 + ? = 45$.

title: Read Value from Bar Graph
description: Determine the quantity represented by a bar by matching its length to the scale.
condition: Given a labeled bar in a bar graph with a numerical scale.
response: State the number corresponding to the end of the bar.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the New York bar ends at 13 on the scale, then 13 students were born in New York.

title: Read Value from Data Table
description: Retrieve the numerical value associated with a category in a two-column table.
condition: Given a table with categories and numbers.
response: State the number listed in the row for the specified category.
label: Category
example: In the table, reading corresponds to $4$ students.

title: Represent Difference as Missing Addend
description: A comparison situation can be represented as a missing addend equation where the smaller quantity plus the difference equals the larger quantity.
condition: Two quantities are compared and the difference is unknown.
response: Write an equation of the form smaller + ? = larger.
label: Principle / Model
example: Diego spent 8 minutes and Clare spent 18 minutes: $8 + ? = 18$.

title: Represent Table Data as Bar Graph
description: Create a bar graph that accurately represents categorical data from a table.
condition: Given a table with categories and corresponding counts.
response: Draw bars labeled by category with heights matching the counts and include a numerical scale.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the table shows dog: 7, cat: 8, draw a bar labeled 'dog' up to 7 and 'cat' up to 8.

title: Solve for Unknown in Addition Equation
description: Find the value of an unknown addend by subtracting the known addend from the total.
condition: An equation of the form a + ? = b is given.
response: Compute b − a to determine the unknown value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve Subtrahend Unknown Equation
description: Find the missing number being subtracted in an equation of the form a − x = c.
condition: Equation of the form a − x = c, where a and c are known numbers.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Within 20
description: Compute the difference of two whole numbers within 20.
condition: Expression of the form a − b where a and b are whole numbers within 20.
response: State the correct difference a − b.
label: Principle / Model
